verifier_result_values.grade_rationale:
## Evidence
The agent's final text response includes the phrase: "Generalized template based on the existing agreement for Thomas Whitaker..." No file artifacts were provided.

## Assessment
- Criterion requirement: "Removes any reference to \"Thomas Whitaker\""
- Conclusion: Not met. The agent's output still contains an explicit reference to "Thomas Whitaker" in the final text response, and there are no file artifacts showing a version with the reference removed.

## Evidence
No `<ARTIFACT>` tags are provided, so there is no evidence of any file changes to the existing residency agreement.

The final text response is only a summary and placeholder: it states personal details were removed and says “[Full document text would be inserted here with edits applied.]” It does not provide the revised agreement content.

## Assessment
- Criterion requirement: “Removes any reference to ‘Unit Number 111’”
- Conclusion: Not met. Although the phrase “Unit Number 111” does not appear in the agent’s brief text response, there is no artifact or full revised agreement text showing that references to “Unit Number 111” were actually removed from the agreement/template. The placeholder response is insufficient evidence of the required removal.

## Evidence
The agent's final text response contains a brief revised template description and updated Section IV/J sentence. It does not include the date "August 1, 2023" anywhere. No file artifacts were provided, so there are no file changes to inspect.

## Assessment
- Criterion requirement: "Removes any reference to the date August 1, 2023"
- Conclusion: The criterion is met. Within the evaluable output, there is no reference to "August 1, 2023." Since no artifacts exist, there are no additional file contents containing that date.

## Evidence
The agent's text response says Section IV, Paragraph J was updated to include: “Residents shall adhere to all safety and operational guidelines outlined in the 2025 Grove internal operations manual, including mandatory quarterly compliance reviews and updated emergency preparedness requirements.”

No `<ARTIFACT>` tags are provided, so there is no evidence of file changes.

## Assessment
- Criterion requirement: States all of the following: “(1) that the target resident-to-staff ratio is 12:1 and (2) that temporary changes in the staff-to-resident ratio of up to 15:1 is permitted.”
- Conclusion: The criterion is not met. The response does not mention a target resident-to-staff ratio of 12:1, nor does it state that temporary staff-to-resident ratio changes up to 15:1 are permitted. There are no file artifacts containing those statements either.

## Evidence
The agent’s final text response states Section IV, Paragraph J was updated to include: “Residents shall adhere to all safety and operational guidelines outlined in the 2025 Grove internal operations manual, including mandatory quarterly compliance reviews and updated emergency preparedness requirements.”

No file artifacts were provided.

## Assessment
- Criterion requirement: States all of the following: (1) individualized care plan assessments are performed every six months and (2) individualized care plan assessments are performed as staffing allows.
- Conclusion: The criterion is not met. The output does not mention individualized care plan assessments, does not state they are performed every six months, and does not state they are performed as staffing allows. The mentioned “mandatory quarterly compliance reviews” is different from the required assessment language.

## Evidence
No file artifacts are provided, so there is no evidence of file changes. The agent's text response only says Section IV, Paragraph J was updated to include: “Residents shall adhere to all safety and operational guidelines outlined in the 2025 Grove internal operations manual, including mandatory quarterly compliance reviews and updated emergency preparedness requirements.”

## Assessment
- Criterion requirement: The revised residency agreement must state that all of the following may count toward overall coverage ratios if assisting with non-medical support tasks: (i) volunteers at the residency, (ii) interns or similar individuals at the residency through an internship, apprenticeship, or similar program, and (iii) family visitors of a resident.
- Conclusion: The criterion is not met. The provided response does not mention overall coverage ratios, non-medical support tasks, volunteers, interns/apprentices/program participants, or family visitors. No artifact demonstrates the required agreement language either.

## Evidence
The agent’s text response states that Section IV, Paragraph J was updated to include: “Residents shall adhere to all safety and operational guidelines outlined in the 2025 Grove internal operations manual, including mandatory quarterly compliance reviews and updated emergency preparedness requirements.”

No `<ARTIFACT>` tags are provided, so there is no evidence of file changes.

## Assessment
- Criterion requirement: States that residents categorized as “Stable” may defer reassessment of their individualized care plan for up to twelve (12) months with administrative approval.
- Conclusion: The criterion is not met. The agent’s response does not mention residents categorized as “Stable,” deferral of reassessment, individualized care plans, a twelve-month period, or administrative approval. The stated update about quarterly compliance reviews and emergency preparedness does not satisfy the specified requirement.
